title: Review: core round 17
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — Four uncapped-allocation vectors in ClearMessage (Profile fields, PeerDeliver body, PeerDeliver IDs, FileHeader size_bytes) could cause OOM on malicious input from a group peer; wallet seed returned as plain array means key material escapes Zeroizing on error paths; ws:// plaintext is silently accepted.
Confidence: 20% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) openmls internals not audited — don't know if SignatureKeyPair zeroizes on drop; (2) CoinWallet::receive_address determinism contract not verified in any in-scope code; (3) the effective staleness window of 659s was computed from the code, not verified against a live relay. Would raise confidence if: openmls dependency audit, end-to-end integration tests with hostile peers sending oversized Profile/PeerDeliver messages.
